problem_statement.fact: The backend pool expansion analyzer routed all five rows to request_backend_candidate_generation because the available paper-scale candidate artifact contains only 10 candidates per row.
problem_statement.agent_inference: The next branch should first materialize a fixed top-20 non-GT candidate pool from existing action-time semantic evidence before launching deeper dense re-export or goal-validity confirmation.
problem_statement.paper_constraint: This contract is candidate generation and accounting only. It must not be promoted as terminal ObjectNav utility or as evidence that a generated candidate is a valid goal.

fixed_generation_policy.spatial_diversity_source_required: candidate ids must come from the existing spatial_nms candidate artifact or report why that lineage is unavailable
fixed_generation_policy.reachability_policy: do not filter out unknown-reachability candidates at generation time; report reachable, unreachable, and unknown counts separately
fixed_generation_policy.duplicate_policy: duplicate candidate ids are forbidden; duplicate or near-duplicate positions must be reported

allowed_action_inputs: backend expansion rows with backend_route_action == request_backend_candidate_generation
episode_key, scene_key, query, expanded_retrieval_request_id, and rival_identity_request_id
source action evidence candidate ids, semantic rank, semantic score, support score, positive support, position, visit_position, reachability, and path-to-candidate fields
candidate lineage fields showing whether candidates came from spatial_nms or another fixed backend
navmesh or standoff feasibility fields generated without target labels
forbidden_action_inputs: choosing top-k, backend family, or route action after inspecting per-row evaluation correctness

candidate_generation_objective.required_question: Can a fixed non-GT top-20 backend candidate pool be materialized with accountable candidate lineage, duplicate/reachability accounting, and no GT action inputs for the five backend expansion rows?

candidate_generation_statuses.condition: exactly 20 non-GT candidates are materialized from source action evidence with finite candidate positions and no duplicate candidate ids
fixed top-20 source action evidence is unavailable, incomplete, duplicate-heavy, nonfinite, or still insufficient after evaluation-only reporting
required source action evidence or candidate lineage is unavailable and deeper generation cannot be launched under the current workflow

candidate_pool_accounting: report generated candidate ids and source candidate ids
report generated, retained-from-top10, new-beyond-top10, duplicate, finite-position, reachable, unknown-reachability, and positive-support counts
report whether source-top, detector-strong, local-context, and previous action candidates remain in the generated pool
report candidate budget, backend family, selection order, and deterministic sort key
lineage_and_reproducibility: record upstream backend expansion row path
record candidate backend family and spatial_nms lineage when available
record exact Docker command for analyzer implementation
evaluation_only_reporting: join evaluation labels only after candidate_generation rows are written
report contains-valid and no-valid generated pools by generation status
do not use evaluation labels to choose candidate budget, backend family, route status, or threshold

expected_next_analyzer.name: expanded_retrieval_backend_candidate_generation_v1
expected_next_analyzer.role: materialize fixed top-20 backend candidate generation rows for request_backend_candidate_generation rows and report whether deeper generation or goal-validity confirmation should be considered next
expected_next_analyzer.input_scope: five rows from h001_expanded_retrieval_backend_pool_expansion_v1 with backend_route_action == request_backend_candidate_generation

promotion_rules.contract_only: Passing this contract permits implementation of fixed backend candidate generation, not terminal ObjectNav commitment.
promotion_rules.goal_validity_confirmation_unblocked: Goal-validity confirmation can be defined only after generated pools are structurally valid and post-generation analysis does not show unresolved no-valid pool contamination, or after a non-GT validity proxy can isolate no-valid pools without labels.
promotion_rules.deeper_generation_unblocked: If fixed top-20 generation still leaves no-valid pools after evaluation-only reporting, define a separate deeper backend generation contract before goal-validity confirmation.
promotion_rules.paper_claim_blocked: No policy-scale or terminal ObjectNav claim is allowed until candidate generation and goal-validity confirmation pass on a predeclared source.

next_steps: Implement backend candidate generation analyzer from this contract.
Run Docker py_compile or equivalent smoke check.
Run the analyzer on the five frozen request_backend_candidate_generation rows.
Join evaluation labels only after writing action-time generation rows.
Use the result to decide whether the next contract is deeper backend generation or goal-validity confirmation.
